headline: BREAKING: Wonder Wharf Acquires Arozarena as North Correa Gambles on Rotation Depth at the Midseason Crunch
body:
**Randy Arozarena** is heading to Wonder Wharf. **Dustin May** and **Michael Harris II** are heading to North Correa. The deal is done, sources familiar with the situation confirm, and both teams are betting their second-half survival on getting this right.

Start with the math on Wonder Wharf (3-8-3, 50 PF, 13th). Dead last among active contenders, they are not rebuilding — they are drowning. **Arozarena** is a direct injection into their most exploitable weaknesses: SB, OBP, and raw hit production in H/AB. Playoff relevance requires a sustained winning streak starting now, and a healthy **Arozarena** can move the needle in three categories simultaneously. The Wonderdogs needed a name. They got one.

North Correa (5-7-2, 65 PF, 9th) is in harder territory to read. They sit on the wrong side of the playoff bubble with just enough points-for to suggest the roster has real pop — 65 PF is not a cellar-dweller number. What they are betting is that **Dustin May** stabilizes their ERA and WHIP while adding IP and QS volume, and that **Harris II** covers SB and OBP ground **Arozarena** vacated. It is a reasonable theory. It is also two players replacing one, which is never a clean exchange.

The verdict: Wonder Wharf wins this trade short-term — **Arozarena** is the most immediately impactful player changing hands. North Correa wins it long-term only if **May** stays healthy and pitches deep into starts, a qualification that carries real risk attached to it.

At 3-8-3 and Week 14, Wonder Wharf needed a lifeline. Sources familiar with their situation confirm they just grabbed one.
